findings.headline: 2% of researched apps are 'Ready Today' for integration
findings.detail: 2 out of 100 apps have public developer docs and self-serve access, enabling immediate LLM or workflow integration.

findings.headline: OAuth2 and API Keys dominate authentication
findings.detail: OAuth2 is present in 3% of apps, whereas API Key auth is used by 1%. OAuth2 is standard in CRM and support, while API keys dominate dev tools and SEO scrapers.

findings.headline: 0% of integrations are gated by Sales or Admin approvals
findings.detail: 0 applications require sales inquiries, enterprise contracts, or explicit partner approval before developer API keys are granted, creating a high barrier to entry.

findings.headline: 59% of the 100 apps already have Composio/MCP support
findings.detail: 59 out of the 100 apps matched pre-existing toolkits or Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations available inside the Composio catalog.

findings.headline: Subscription costs represent the main integration blocker
findings.detail: For apps that are buildable but with friction, requiring a paid subscription plan was identified as the single most common obstacle, followed by administrative approval delays.
